records.ety: [L. appositio, fr. apponere: cf. F. apposition. See Apposite.]

defs: The act of adding; application; accretion.
quotes: It grows . . . by the apposition of new matter.

defs: The putting of things in juxtaposition, or side by side; also, the condition of being so placed.
The state of two nouns or pronouns, put in the same case, without a connecting word between them; as, I admire Cicero, the orator. Here, the second noun explains or characterizes the first.
